content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To: Dwight Dickerson, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

From: Office of the Attorney General

Subject: Gifts of Tickets - Reporting Requirements

Department of Justice

Date: September 14, 1978

File No.:

Telephone: ATSS ( )

This is to confirm our telephone conversation today in which
you advised that public employees would not be required to
report as gifts or income tickets to a political fund raising
dinner purchased by a corporation and given to the public employee,
even when the cost of the ticket exceeded $25.00.

Moreover, the corporation purchasing the fund raising tickets would
not be required to report the gift of the tickets given to any
public employees.

In the event your office has a change in position, please let me
know immediately in order that our client may be notified before
the event takes place.

Thank you for your assistance in this matter.

CONSTANCE M. BARTON
Deputy Attorney General

CM:ital

sections.question_synthetic: Whether public employees must report as gifts or income tickets to a political fundraising dinner purchased by a corporation, and whether the corporation must report the gift of such tickets.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming correspondence from Attorney General's office to FPPC, not an FPPC advice letter response. No formal Q/A sections present. Document confirms FPPC's oral position rather than requesting advice. Date and year inferred from document ID (1978).

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether public employees must report as gifts or income tickets to a political fundraising dinner purchased by a corporation, and whether the corporation must report the gift of such tickets.
embedding.first_500_words: This is a memorandum from the Office of the Attorney General to Dwight Dickerson, Esq., Fair Political Practices Commission, regarding gifts of tickets and reporting requirements. The memo states: This is to confirm our telephone conversation today in which you advised that public employees would not be required to report as gifts or income tickets to a political fund raising dinner purchased by a corporation and given to the public employee, even when the cost of the ticket exceeded $25.00. Moreover, the corporation purchasing the fund raising tickets would not be required to report the gift of the tickets given to any public employees. In the event your office has a change in position, please let me know immediately in order that our client may be notified before the event takes place. Thank you for your assistance in this matter. CONSTANCE M. BARTON Deputy Attorney General CMB:tal
embedding.summary: A memorandum from the Office of the Attorney General to the FPPC confirming a telephone conversation in which the FPPC advised that public employees need not report political fundraising dinner tickets as gifts or income (even if exceeding $25), and corporations need not report such gifts to public employees.
